symptoms.symptom_name: Exertional heavy/squeezing retrosternal chest tightness relieved by rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertional chest tightness relieved by rest is the defining symptom of angina.

symptoms.symptom_name: Radiation to the ulnar aspect of the left arm

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Arm radiation is a classic feature of cardiac ischaemic pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Predictable, reproducible exertional threshold, unchanged over 4 weeks, with no pain at rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A fixed, reproducible threshold unchanged over weeks with no rest pain is the hallmark distinguishing STABLE angina from unstable angina.
Absence of crescendo/rest pain and a stable threshold argue against instability, supporting a non-emergency baseline.

symptoms.symptom_name: Exertional breathlessness concurrent with pain; cold-weather provocation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertional dyspnoea as an anginal equivalent and cold-induced provocation support an ischaemic cardiac cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypertension and hyperlipidaemia on treatment (cardiovascular risk factors)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypertension and hyperlipidaemia drive coronary atherosclerosis and raise the pre-test probability of coronary disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain not reproducible on palpation and not related to meals/posture

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-reproducible, non-positional, non-meal-related pain argues against a musculoskeletal or reflux cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal cardiac auscultation at rest (no murmur, no S3/S4)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal resting cardiac exam neither confirms nor excludes stable angina and helps exclude overt heart failure/valvular disease; it is clinician-elicited and not obtainable over telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transient ST depression on exercise stress testing / reversible ischaemia on functional-anatomical coronary testing

investigation_details.investigation_name: Resting 12-lead ECG plus exercise tolerance test or CT coronary angiography
investigation_details.expected_result: Resting ECG often normal between episodes; transient ST depression anticipated on exercise stress testing, or a flow-limiting coronary stenosis on CT coronary angiography
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Stable angina cannot be confirmed or risk-stratified and progressive coronary disease may be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reversible/inducible ischaemia on stress testing or a flow-limiting stenosis on angiography confirms coronary ischaemic disease.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Predictable exertional arm-radiating chest tightness relieved by rest, with a reproducible threshold unchanged over 4 weeks and no rest pain, in a hypertensive/hyperlipidaemic patient, indicates stable angina requiring prompt fast-track cardiac workup and interim GTN/aspirin, with ACS safety-netting.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Baseline screen for ischaemic changes; may be normal between episodes but should be done promptly.
Demonstrates inducible ischaemia (transient ST depression) to confirm stable angina and stratify risk.
Assesses coronary anatomy and flow-limiting stenosis as an alternative/adjunct to functional testing.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Fasting lipids and blood pressure review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Optimise modifiable cardiovascular risk factors alongside cardiac assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate emergency department transfer / ambulance for this currently stable pattern
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A pain-free, haemodynamically stable patient with an unchanged 4-week predictable pattern does not need emergency transport now; prompt outpatient workup with ACS safety-netting is appropriate.
